summary: Backend interest + category-aware broadcast fan-out: 15/15 tests passing (incl. 6 new tests for /api/auth/interests and write-time category filtering). PublicUser exposes interests=[] for new users; AuthGate routes them to /onboarding-interests correctly. Onboarding flow (select interest -> continue -> (tabs)) verified end-to-end via Playwright. CRITICAL frontend bug: Calendar screen RED-SCREENS with ReferenceError: filter is not defined at app/(tabs)/calendar.tsx:125 -- the `filter` state/setter was never declared via useState. This blocks ALL calendar usage and was reproduced for both admin and regular users.

ui_bugs.issue: RED SCREEN: 'ReferenceError: filter is not defined' at line 125. CalendarScreen uses `filter` and passes `setFilter` to <FilterChips/>, but never declares them via useState. Add: const [filter, setFilter] = useState<CategoryKey | 'all'>('all');

TypeScript: `sheet` state union (lines 110-120) is missing the 'category' literal even though setSheet('category') is called and <SelectSheet visible={sheet === 'category'} /> is rendered. Runtime works (TS stripped by Metro), but tsc/strict checks will flag. Add 'category' to the union.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot validate filter chip behavior or category-based filtered display because the screen crashes immediately on mount. Once the `filter` state is added, retest filter-chip-all and filter-chip-<key> selectors plus event visibility on the calendar.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warnings: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow"' and 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Reference: app/sign-in.tsx (LinearGradient pointerEvents='none') and theme shadow helpers.
Expo CLI reports expected upgrades: expo@~54.0.35 (have 54.0.34), expo-font@~14.0.12, expo-router@~6.0.24. Non-blocking but flagged at every restart.

action_items: FIX (1-line): In /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/calendar.tsx add `const [filter, setFilter] = useState<CategoryKey | 'all'>('all');` near the other useState calls (top of CalendarScreen). Default to 'all' so 'Hepsi' chip is preselected.
FIX (types): In /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/admin.tsx add 'category' to the `sheet` state union type so strict TS compiles cleanly.
Replace deprecated 'shadow*' style props with `boxShadow` in src/theme (or any StyleSheet that emits shadow*).
Optionally align expo / expo-router / expo-font to the versions Expo SDK 54 expects.
critical_code_review_comments: calendar.tsx: filter state is referenced but never created -- pure RCE that ships a red screen. Caught only by runtime mount; lint/tsc would have flagged with proper config.
Backend filter is correctly write-time (events not delivered to non-matching users), confirmed by category-aware fan-out test: A=internship -> 1 row, B=job -> 0 rows, C=all -> 1 row.

Onboarding routing in _layout.tsx is sound: admin bypasses (because is_admin true), regular user with empty interests routes to /onboarding-interests, after PUT /auth/interests + refresh() AuthGate re-evaluates and replaces to /(tabs).

success_rate.frontend: 50% (sign-in + onboarding work; calendar crashes; admin tab unreachable through tabs while calendar lazy-loads in tab nav)

seed_data_creation: Test users with TEST_ prefix (TEST_a_*, TEST_b_*, TEST_c_*, TEST_int_*, TEST_new_*, TEST_pu_*, TEST_disc_*, TEST_ui_new@calsync.app) created in MongoDB via /api/auth/google/mobile. Admin: admin@calsync.app.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After E1 adds `const [filter, setFilter] = useState<CategoryKey | 'all'>('all');` to calendar.tsx, retest: (a) calendar-screen renders, (b) filter-chip-all selected by default, (c) tapping filter-chip-internship hides events whose category != internship and 'Hepsi' restores them, (d) admin admin-category-row opens a SelectSheet with 'Staj','İş arıyorum',... and selection updates the broadcast payload, (e) admin Year/Month/Week toggle still works. Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/ are stable -- rerun with `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`.
rca_of_issue: calendar.tsx imports CategoryKey and uses `filter`/`setFilter` inside FilterChips + filteredEvents memo, but the useState declaration was omitted during refactor. Reproduction: open /(tabs) as any user, navigate to Calendar tab -> RedBox 'filter is not defined'. Mitigation: add the missing useState; until then the entire calendar tab is unusable and event details / week view cannot be exercised.
